contains: opens a register and rejects a concurrent second open on the same register
computes zero, short, and over cash variance and gates approval only on non-zero
denies self-approval of a cash variance and allows a different approver to close it
replays an idempotent open and cash-movement command without duplicating effects
validates money and direction/reason-code pairing at the domain boundary
applies a per-line discount before computing tax
rejects sale creation when the register has no open session
rejects completion when tendered cash is less than the sale total
holds an Open sale and completes it after hold via implicit resume
replays an idempotent sale.create and sale.complete without duplicating a receipt or stock movement
reads back a persisted receipt by id
rejects a non-Cash tender politely at the completion boundary (WS4/WS6 scope)
uses Pending -> Completed for a return and Requested -> Posted for a refund, never pre-approved
prevents an over-return, cumulative across two partial returns
rejects a return referencing a sale that has not yet completed
rejects a refund approval once the referenced register's session is no longer open
rejects reissuing a Reissue receipt
rejects voiding a non-Sale receipt
rejects an exchange whose return is still Pending
derives a refund's amount solely from the Return, never from caller input
Prepared precedes Reconciled and preparation never starts confirmed
prepares a deposit that reserves safe custody and posts NO custody transfer
rejects a deposit that exceeds available safe custody
caps cumulative reservation across two deposits against the same safe-drop pool
denies self-confirmation by the preparer
rejects confirming an already-Reconciled deposit
idempotency-key replay returns the identical prepared and confirmed result
checks permission before dispatching to the service
dispatches to the service once permission and entitlement are granted
application layer requires permission and denies self-confirmation before dispatch
rejects a genuine concurrent double-open race on the same register via the DB partial unique index
writes the opened-register outbox row atomically in the owning transaction (raw SQL)
rolls back the register-session row when the outbox append fails in the same transaction
isolates two tenants sharing the same register id: cannot read or close each other's session
denies before dispatch when permission is not granted, and dispatches once granted
replays a cash-movement command idempotently and rejects a conflicting body reusing the same key
routes a non-zero cash variance through maker/checker approval end-to-end with the DB row
completes a cash sale atomically: sale row, numbered receipt, synchronous Inventory movement, and both outbox events commit together (raw SQL)
rolls back the sale, receipt, receipt-number allocation, and Inventory movement together when an injected failure occurs before commit (raw SQL)
completes a sale exactly once under a genuine 10-way concurrent idempotent replay race
issues monotonic, non-duplicate receipt numbers per register under genuine concurrent sale completions
isolates two tenants sharing the same register id: cannot read each other's sale or receipt, and receipt numbering never leaks across tenants
denies sale completion before dispatch when permission is not granted, and dispatches once granted
measures add-scanned-item lookup and platform sale-processing latency against the named governing targets, with retained samples and an explicit disposition
completes return -> refund atomically end to end: Return/lines, compensating Inventory movement, Return receipt, Refund, cash movement, and every outbox event commit together (raw SQL)
posts two SEPARATE compensating movements for two sequential partial returns against the SAME original sale movement (Inventory's per-original one-reversal cap is scoped to Adjustment, not Sale)
rolls back the compensating Inventory movement, Return receipt, and Return state together when an injected failure occurs before commit (raw SQL)
prevents an over-return under a genuine concurrent double-return race: the cumulative cap holds across both attempts
replays a return-approval command idempotently under a genuine concurrent race: exactly one compensating movement, one receipt
keeps returns strictly tenant-scoped: tenant B cannot reference tenant A's sale, and cannot see or approve tenant A's return
denies return approval before application dispatch when permission is not granted, and dispatches once granted
realizes an exchange atomically: the replacement sale completes, the Return is marked consumed, and commerce.exchange.completed.v1 commits together with the sale (raw SQL)
voids a completed sale on an open session atomically, and rejects the void once the register session has closed
reissues a receipt with no monetary effect, and priceSuppressed realizes a gift receipt
prepares a deposit with NO custody-transfer row (effect-free preparation, raw SQL)
posts exactly one custody-transfer row on confirm, atomic with the state transition
concurrent double-confirm of the same deposit: exactly one posts (round-3 P1-4)
concurrent preparations over-reserving the same safe balance: the cumulative cap holds (round-3 P1-4)
idempotency-key replay on prepare returns the identical deposit, inserting no second row
idempotency-key replay on confirm returns the identical result, posting no second custody transfer
rejects a deposit that exceeds available safe custody (live-PG custody conservation)
denies self-confirmation through the application layer and never dispatches
completeness reconciliation: exported control totals equal independently-computed raw SQL sums
cross-tenant isolation: an export for one tenant never includes another tenant's sales, and reads deny across tenants
date-boundary correctness: a sale at local midnight (America/Guyana, UTC-4) lands in the correct local day

keeps refund and deposit strictly tenant-scoped: tenant B cannot reference or approve/confirm tenant A's refund or deposit
denies deposit creation and confirmation through the application layer before dispatch, and dispatches once granted
records bounded register-open and zero-variance register-close command samples (performance_and_capacity, real reproduced numbers)
records bounded return-approval, refund-approval, and deposit-confirmation command samples (performance_and_capacity, real reproduced numbers)

a commerce.sale.completed.v1 event with no registered consumer is marked delivered exactly once via the no_consumers disposition, proving no worker-side stock-movement path exists to double-move anything on redelivery

duplicate commerce.sale.completed.v1 delivery (two separately-appended rows for a retried publish) both resolve no_consumers with zero consumer executions and zero duplicate business effects, since no worker consumer path exists to duplicate
dispatches openRegister only after permission enforcement, deriving locationId from the active context
denies register open before application dispatch when permission fails
dispatches sale completion with the saleId and flattened cash tenders from the request
denies sale completion before application dispatch when permission fails
dispatches return creation with the sale, lines, and reason from the request body
dispatches refund approval with the refundId from the request path
dispatches deposit creation with the currency, countedAmount, and sourceShiftIds from the request body
realizes commerce.exchanges by threading exchangeOfReturnId from completeSale's request body (frozen control plan §6.5)
dispatches receipt reissue with priceSuppressed from the request body (gift-receipt variant)
formats GYD minor units as currency
changeDueMinor never returns negative
canCompleteSale blocks completion while an override is Pending (frozen control plan §6.2)
ledgerEntryMinor signs paid-out, safe-drop, and refund negative
full cash sale: open register, complete a sale, view the receipt, close the register at zero variance
sale hold and resume: a Held sale can be completed after navigating away and back
price-override request on a sale line, approved by a second identity, unblocks completion
return flow: creating a return against a completed sale leaves it Pending with no inventory effect yet
return approval by a second identity (approver != creator) posts the compensating inventory movement
refund create and approve: a second identity's approval posts the paid-out cash movement
deposit create and confirm: a second identity's confirmation reconciles the deposit
receipt reissue produces a new numbered artifact
receipt void reverses the sale through the receipt view

accessibility: the return-creation, refund-request, and deposit-preparation landing forms have no automated WCAG A/AA violations
online-only WS3 mutations fail closed and remain understandable when connectivity drops
handoff export: generating an accountant export triggers a real download
